content.text: GenAI systems depend on assets that expire: model versions get deprecated by providers, prompt templates accumulate technical debt and must be replaced, and eval benchmarks go stale. Without a formal sunset notification process, teams discover these expirations under pressure — scrambling to migrate during an incident or after a provider has already pulled the API endpoint. A structured notification process converts reactive fire drills into planned migrations.

attrs.alt: Sunset notification timeline: provider deprecation at T-90, internal team notification at T-60, migration code deployed at T-30, full traffic cut-over at T-7, and provider sunset at T=0

content.text: Two Types of Sunsets
The sunset notification process handles two distinct scenarios that require different workflows:
Type
Trigger
Who drives the timeline
Migration window
Provider-driven model deprecation
Provider announces end-of-life for a model version
Provider sets the deadline; team must meet it
Typically 90–180 days
Internal prompt/API version sunset
Team decides an old prompt version or internal API is no longer supported
Team controls the timeline
Team-defined; typically 60 days

The process described here covers both. Provider-driven deprecations are harder because the deadline is external and non-negotiable; internal sunsets allow more flexibility but require discipline to avoid indefinite support of obsolete versions.
Provider-Driven Model Deprecation
Monitoring for Announcements
Provider deprecation notices arrive through multiple channels and with varying lead times. Waiting for a developer to notice a blog post is not a process — it is luck:

import re
import smtplib
from dataclasses import dataclass
from email.message import EmailMessage
from datetime import date

@dataclass
class DeprecationNotice:
    model_id: str
    sunset_date: date
    successor_model: str | None
    source_url: str
    announced_date: date

def parse_anthropic_deprecation_email(raw_email: str) -> DeprecationNotice | None:
    """
    Parse structured deprecation notice from provider email.
    Adapt pattern to match actual provider email format.
    """
    model_pattern = re.search(r'model[:\s]+([\w.-]+)', raw_email, re.IGNORECASE)
    date_pattern  = re.search(r'(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})', raw_email)
    successor_pat = re.search(r'successor[:\s]+([\w.-]+)', raw_email, re.IGNORECASE)

    if not model_pattern or not date_pattern:
        return None

    return DeprecationNotice(
        model_id=model_pattern.group(1),
        sunset_date=date.fromisoformat(date_pattern.group(1)),
        successor_model=successor_pat.group(1) if successor_pat else None,
        source_url="",
        announced_date=date.today(),
    )

def check_model_endpoint_health(model_id: str, provider_client) -> bool:
    """
    Nightly health check: attempt a minimal call to confirm the model
    endpoint still responds. Alerts before provider actually pulls it.
    """
    try:
        resp = provider_client.messages.create(
            model=model_id,
            max_tokens=1,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "ping"}],
        )
        return True
    except Exception as e:
        if "deprecated" in str(e).lower() or "not found" in str(e).lower():
            fire_alert(
                channel="#ai-ops",
                message=f"Model {model_id!r} endpoint returning error: {e}. "
                        "Immediate migration check required.",
                severity="critical",
            )
        return False
The 90-Day Response Workflow
Once a deprecation notice is received (or detected by the nightly health check), the team follows a four-phase response:
T−90: Audit and plan.

Run an automated scan to identify every prompt template, config file, and integration that references the deprecated model ID. Create a Jira epic with one task per reference. Draft the migration plan — typically this means evaluating the successor model against the existing eval set.
T−60: Evaluate the successor.

Run the successor model through the full fitness suite. If quality, latency, or cost fitness results are outside acceptable bounds, flag immediately — the team has 60 days to either accept the degradation with documented justification or find an alternative successor.
T−30: Deploy the migration behind a feature flag.
Merge the updated model reference, config, and prompt adjustments behind a flag. Run a canary deployment at 5% traffic. Monitor for 7 days before expanding.
T−7: Complete full traffic migration.
Remove the deprecated model reference from all production code. Update runbooks, dashboards, and eval baselines. Confirm no remaining references in configuration or code with a final audit scan.
Internal Prompt Version Sunset

When a team replaces a prompt template with an improved version, the old version should not live indefinitely — it accumulates as technical debt, and callers that never migrated are silently receiving degraded behavior. A formal internal sunset process prevents this:
Versioning Prompt Templates
Prompt templates that are used by more than one service should be versioned explicitly, with a lifecycle state tracked in the prompt registry:

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date
from typing import Literal

PromptLifecycle = Literal["active", "deprecated", "sunset"]

@dataclass
class PromptVersion:
    prompt_id: str
    version: str                     # e.g. "v2.1"
    lifecycle: PromptLifecycle = "active"
    deprecated_date: date | None = None
    sunset_date: date | None = None
    successor_version: str | None = None
    deprecation_reason: str = ""

    def deprecate(
        self,
        successor: str,
        reason: str,
        migration_window_days: int = 60,
    ) -> None:
        if self.lifecycle != "active":
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot deprecate a prompt in state {self.lifecycle!r}")
        self.lifecycle = "deprecated"
        self.deprecated_date = date.today()
        self.sunset_date = date.today() + __import__("datetime").timedelta(days=migration_window_days)
        self.successor_version = successor
        self.deprecation_reason = reason

    def sunset(self) -> None:
        if self.lifecycle != "deprecated":
            raise ValueError("Can only sunset a deprecated prompt")
        if self.sunset_date and date.today() < self.sunset_date:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Sunset date is {self.sunset_date} — too early to sunset. "
                "Use emergency_sunset() to override with on-call approval."
            )
        self.lifecycle = "sunset"

    def emergency_sunset(self, approver: str) -> None:
        """Override sunset date — requires async on-call approval."""
        self.lifecycle = "sunset"
        self.deprecation_reason += f" [Emergency sunset approved by {approver}]"
Notifying Callers of Prompt Deprecation

When a prompt version moves to deprecated state, every service that has called that version in the past 30 days receives a deprecation notice. The notice is delivered in two ways: a Slack alert to the owning team, and a deprecation header in the API response itself:

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class PromptResponse:
    text: str
    prompt_id: str
    prompt_version: str
    deprecation_warning: str | None = None

class PromptRegistry:
    def get(self, prompt_id: str, version: str) -> "PromptVersion": ...
    def get_callers(self, prompt_id: str, version: str, days: int) -> list[str]: ...

def call_prompt(
    registry: PromptRegistry,
    prompt_id: str,
    version: str,
    variables: dict,
    llm_client,
) -> PromptResponse:
    prompt_version = registry.get(prompt_id, version)

    warning = None
    if prompt_version.lifecycle == "deprecated":
        days_left = (prompt_version.sunset_date - __import__("datetime").date.today()).days
        warning = (
            f"Prompt {prompt_id}@{version} is deprecated. "
            f"Migrate to {prompt_version.successor_version} within {days_left} days. "
            f"Reason: {prompt_version.deprecation_reason}"
        )
    elif prompt_version.lifecycle == "sunset":
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"Prompt {prompt_id}@{version} has been sunset. "
            f"Use {prompt_version.successor_version} instead. "
            "See runbook: https://wiki/prompt-migration"
        )

    rendered = prompt_version.render(variables)
    response = llm_client.complete(rendered)
    return PromptResponse(
        text=response.text,
        prompt_id=prompt_id,
        prompt_version=version,
        deprecation_warning=warning,
    )
Automated Deprecation Enforcement

Deprecation notices in response headers only work if callers read them. A more reliable enforcement mechanism is to route deprecated prompt calls through a slow-path that adds measurable latency — not enough to break the application, but enough to create an operational incentive to migrate:

import asyncio

DEPRECATION_PENALTY_MS = 200  # added latency on deprecated prompt calls

async def call_prompt_async(
    registry,
    prompt_id: str,
    version: str,
    variables: dict,
    llm_client,
) -> PromptResponse:
    prompt_version = registry.get(prompt_id, version)

    if prompt_version.lifecycle == "deprecated":
        # Measurable latency penalty surfaces in SLO dashboards
        await asyncio.sleep(DEPRECATION_PENALTY_MS / 1000)
        log_deprecation_usage(prompt_id, version)

    elif prompt_version.lifecycle == "sunset":
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"Prompt {prompt_id}@{version} is sunset. "
            f"Successor: {prompt_version.successor_version}"
        )

    rendered = prompt_version.render(variables)
    response = await llm_client.complete_async(rendered)
    return PromptResponse(
        text=response.text,
        prompt_id=prompt_id,
        prompt_version=version,
        deprecation_warning="Deprecated — migrate to avoid latency penalty" if prompt_version.lifecycle == "deprecated" else None,
    )
Notification Templates

Consistent notification content reduces the time callers spend figuring out what they need to do. The following templates cover the two key moments: initial deprecation notice and final sunset reminder.
Deprecation Notice (T-60 / T-90)

Subject: [ACTION REQUIRED] Model claude-2 deprecated — migrate to claude-sonnet-5 by 2025-09-01

Hi team,

Anthropic has announced that **claude-2** will be retired on **2025-09-01** (90 days from now).

**Impact on your services:**
- Service: `ai-support-bot` (prompt: `support-reply-v3`, line 42 in `config/models.yaml`)
- Service: `doc-summarizer` (prompt: `doc-summary-v1`, line 8 in `config/models.yaml`)

**Recommended successor:** `claude-sonnet-5`

**What you need to do:**
1. Update `config/models.yaml` to reference `claude-sonnet-5`
2. Re-run the eval suite to confirm quality parity: `make run-eval`
3. Deploy behind the `model-migration` feature flag
4. Mark migration complete in Jira: [AIOPS-4312]

**Timeline:**
- 2025-07-01 (T-60): Eval + plan complete
- 2025-08-01 (T-30): Migration merged to main, canary live
- 2025-08-25 (T-7): Full traffic on successor
- 2025-09-01 (T-0): Provider retires claude-2

Questions? Contact #ai-platform or file a ticket against AIOPS-4312.
Final Sunset Reminder (T-7)

Subject: [URGENT] claude-2 retires in 7 days — verify your migration is complete

Hi team,

**claude-2 is retired on 2025-09-01 — 7 days from now.**

Our audit shows the following services still reference claude-2:
- `doc-summarizer` — last call: 2025-08-24 16:42 UTC

If this is already migrated and the reference is stale configuration, please confirm by
updating your deployment config and replying to this thread.

If migration is not complete, escalate to #ai-platform immediately.
After 2025-09-01, calls to claude-2 will fail with HTTP 404.

Runbook for emergency migration: https://wiki/model-migration-emergency
Tracking Sunset Compliance
A sunset notification process is only effective if it has a feedback loop. After each migration cycle, record whether callers completed migration on time:

Review these metrics quarterly. An on-time migration rate below 80% indicates that the notification window is too short, the effort estimation is wrong, or teams lack the operational capacity to absorb migrations alongside feature work.
Summary
A structured sunset notification process eliminates the most common form of GenAI operational debt: unplanned migrations under deadline pressure. The key elements are:
Automated monitoring for provider deprecation notices — do not rely on manual channel scanning.
A four-phase response timeline for provider-driven deprecations (T-90, T-60, T-30, T-7).
Internal prompt versioning with explicit lifecycle states (active → deprecated → sunset).
Programmatic enforcement: deprecation warnings in API responses and measurable latency penalties for deprecated callers.
Consistent notification templates that tell callers exactly what to do and by when.
Migration outcome tracking to improve the process over time.

The process is lightweight enough to run as part of normal platform operations. Its value compounds over time: each sunset handled cleanly builds team confidence and reduces the cognitive overhead of the next one.
